data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: The time it takes to make a decision increases with the number and complexity of choices.
data.technical_spec: Simplified navigation and clear content hierarchy to minimize cognitive load.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: Ease of access to navigation elements based on size and distance from the user.
data.technical_spec: Strategically placed and appropriately sized interactive elements.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: The average person can only keep 7 (plus or minus 2) items in their working memory.
data.technical_spec: Chunking information in manageable sections to enhance user comprehension.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Users spend most of their time on other sites, so they prefer your site to work the same way as all the other sites they already know.
data.technical_spec: Consistency with common web design patterns and familiar UI elements.

data.title: Serial Position Effect
data.description: Users tend to best remember the first and last items in a series.
data.technical_spec: Present critical information at the beginning or end of any process.
